label: Trees?
desc: Parameter governing an option to simulate system with trees. Value 0 means system without trees, value 1 means system with trees is possible

label: Parkland System?
desc: Switch to decide on circular versus linear symmetry. 1 = circular system, 0 =linear system

label: Crops?
desc: Parameter governing an option to simulate system with crop. Value 0 means system without crop, value 1 means system with crop
Equivalent depth of the subsoil below layer 4, that is used to calculate the effective water outflow from the soil column, via S_KsatVDeepSub
Swicth for making the depth of soil layer 1 on sloping land system a dynamic property
Depth of groundwater table below the bottom of layer 4, expressed in m. For the time being the value is used as a constant in defining 'field capacity'.

label: Soil Layer Thickness
desc: Soil depth increment in (= layer thickness of) i-th soil layer, i = 1, 2, 3, 4. For sloping land systems the value for the layer 1 is used as average topsoil depth at the start of the run; actual depth of layer 1 will be calculated from the two AF_Slope parameters

label: Pests?
desc: Switch governing an option to simulate system with dynamic pest impact. Value 0 means no dynamic pest impacts, value 1 means dynamic pest impacts is possible.
Number of similar uphill plot neighbors as source of Lateral Inflow & Run-on

label: Nutrient limitation?
desc: Switch governing an option to simulate system with nutrient limitation. Value 0 means no nutrient limitation, value 1 means nutrient is possible.
Fraction of surface runoff from the area uphill that enters the simulation area as run-on.

label: Water limitation?
desc: Parameter governing an option to simulate system with water limitation. Value 0 means no water limitation, value 1 means water limitation is possible.

label: Are you simulating weed growth?
desc: Parameter governing an option to simulate weed growth. Value 0 means no weed growth, value 1 means weed will start growing whenever crop is absent.
Slope (expressed as percent elevation increment per horizontal distance) of the soil horizons below the surface, especially that of the topsoil, used to calculate actual topsoil depth per zone.

Slope (expressed as percent elevation increment per horizontal distance) of the soil surface at the start of the simulation; this value can differ from the slope of the soil profile AF_SlopeSoilHoriz, but should not differ too much.
Fraction of stone in each soil layer and zone

label: Tree Position Across Zones
desc: Position of each tree type. It can be in zone 1 (1) or zone 4 (4); if one wants it to be in both, two otherwise equal tree types can be defined.
Switch 0 or 1 to have weeding application for each zone (1 = weeding application, 0 = no weeding)
Width of each zone. Width of zone 4 is calculated back from AF_ZoneTot minus the sum of zone 1+2+3

label: Total Zone Width
desc: Total width of agroforestry system simulated
Standard moisture content for expressing marketable yields of each crop
On/Off switch for applying the maintenance respiration,;1 = on, 0 =off
Fraction of the weed seed bank that looses viability and is transferred to the litter pool for decomposition
Effectiveness of crop roots as host for a parasitic tree (T1)
Relative weighting factor for growth used in calculating daily maintenance respiration
Relative weighting factor for roots used in calculating daily maintenance respiration
Relative weighting factor for stem & leaves used in calculating daily maintenance respiration
Relative weighting factor for developing fruits/Yield as part of total biomass as used for maintenance respiration

label: Fraction of Crop Residue removed from field
desc: Fraction of crop residue removed from field (not returned as mulch). The same value applies for all zones and all crops used in the simulation
The relative use of resources for maintenance respiration per unit biomass
A graphical relation between temperature and maintenance respiration

label: Minimum Air Temperature for Crop
desc: Minimum Air Temperature for Crop

label: Optimum Air Temperature for Crop
desc: Optimum Air Temperature for Crop
Fraction of weed seeds in the seed bank that germinates when a new opportunity arises, e.g. at the end of a cropping season
Initial dry weight of weed seeds in seed bank
Daily influx of weed seeds from outside of the plot

Day of year at which simulation starts
Parameter governing an option to have simulation with applying external organic input or not. Value 0 means not applying external organic input, value 1 means applying external organic input
Parameter governing an option to have simulation with applying fertilizer or not. Value 0 means not applying fertilizer, value 1 means applying fertilizer

desc: Amount of crop canopy biomass at which canopy is closed and nutrient demand per unit new biomass shifts from Cq_ConcYoung to Cq_ConcOld.

label: Crop cover efficiency factor
desc: Crop Cover Efficiency factor, used in calculating erosion (Erosion type 1)

desc: Maximum daily dry matter production rate at full light capture, for each crop species under local conditions

desc: Light extinction coefficient for the crop canopy = efficiency of crop foliage in absorbing light.

desc: Lignin concentration of crop residue (eg. 20%=0.2).

desc: Switch (0 = false, 1 = true) to choose between variable (N-stress dependent) versus constant N2 fixation as fraction of N deficit

desc: Plant potential where transpiration is Alpha*potential transpiration, Alpha is a small value (e.g. 0.01). Value could be different depend on crop type.

Decomposition rate of active surface litter pool relative to decomposition rate of active soil fraction pool. It's assumed the decomposition rate of active surface litter and active soil fraction is the same.

Decomposition rate of metabolic surface litter pool relative to decomposition rate of metabolic soil litter pool. It's adopted from century model, the decomposition rate of metabolic surface litter pool = 0.028 per week = 0.04 per day and the decomposition rate of metabolic soil litter pool = 0.35 per week = 0.05 per day.

Decomposition rate of passive surface litter pool relative to decomposition rate of passive soil fraction pool. It's assumed the decomposition rate of passive surface litter and passive soil fraction is the same.

Decomposition rate of slow surface litter pool relative to decomposition rate of slow soil fraction pool. It's assumed the decomposition rate of slow surface litter and slow soil fraction is the same.

Decomposition rate of structural surface litter pool relative to decomposition rate of structural soil litter pool. It's adopted from century model, the decomposition rate of structural surface litter pool = 0.076 per week = 0.010857 per day and the decomposition rate of structural soil litter pool = 0.094 per week = 0.013429 per day.

Coefficient variation of rainfall in mm, rainfall based on tabulated monthly rainfall (Rain_Atype=3)
